---
title: "Episode 2: Sports Personality of the Year — Can we use stats to rank sports?"
date: 2025-12-17
description: "The BBC Sports Personality of the Year award compares athletes from very different sports. Can we use maths to do the same thing, perhaps more fairly?"
categories: [Maths, Statistics, SPOTY]
---

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
**Published**: 2025-12-17  
**Duration**: 34:18  
**YouTube**: [Watch here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fQYV_adrnNI)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
**Topics Covered**: Maths, Statistics, SPOTY
:::

:::

## Video

<iframe width="100%" height="400" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/fQYV_adrnNI?si=kHMKWJdmOZ5DzsS1" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen></iframe>

## Overview

Welcome to the Corridor of Uncertainty, a podcast at the intersection of maths & sport. Hosted by Dr Jess Hargreaves & Dr Rich Bingham, two mathematicians at the University of York.

The BBC Sports Personality of the Year award compares athletes & competitors from very different sports. Can we use maths to do the same thing, maybe in a fairer way? Here we discuss this year's nominations, previous winners (and some omissions) before looking at an attempt to rank all-time sporting greats.

## Key Concepts

::: {.callout-tip}
## \[Key Concept 1\]
\[Explanation of the concept\]
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## \[Key Concept 2\]
\[Explanation of the concept\]
:::

## Interactive Demonstration

\[If applicable, add interactive visualisations here\]

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| eval: false

import plotly.express as px
import pandas as pd

# Replace with actual content from episode
df = pd.DataFrame({
    'x': [1, 2, 3, 4, 5],
    'y': [2, 4, 1, 5, 3]
})

fig = px.line(df, x='x', y='y', title='Example Interactive Plot')
fig.show()
```

## Resources & References

- \[Link to paper/article 1\]
- \[Link to code repository\]
- \[Link to related resource\]

## Transcript Highlights

> "\[Notable quote from the video\]"

\[Additional context or explanation\]

> "\[Another notable quote\]"

\[Context\]

## Code Examples

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Python

```python
# Example code from episode
def example_function():
    pass
```

## R

```r
# R equivalent if applicable
example_function <- function() {
  # code
}
```

:::

## Discussion

\[Add any additional commentary, extended explanations, or connections to other episodes\]

::: {.callout-note}
## Related Episodes
- \[Episode X: Related Topic\](episode-00X.qmd)
- \[Episode Y: Follow-up Topic\](episode-00Y.qmd)
